notes: Test content at test/smoke/spine-skeleton.smoke.ts (named deliverable per ticket). Auto-discovery shim at test/smoke/spine-skeleton.smoke.ts.test.ts enables discovery via `bun test ./src ./test`. Explicit path `bun test test/smoke/spine-skeleton.smoke.ts` runs via substring filter on shim filename. RBG red: BREAK_MATCHER=1 forces all decisions to 'pass' → 0 trace lines → expect(emitted.length).toBe(1) fails (Expected: 1, Received: 0). RBG green: normal run → 1 action decision → 1 trace line → all 12 assertions pass.
RBG red: appended `const _rbgTypeError: number = 'string'` to src/types.ts → TS2322 error, exit 2. RBG green: type error removed → exit 0 (no output, expected clean-pass behavior).

scope: src/** test/** fixtures/** (excludes secret-scan.test.ts which has intentional fake keys)
notes: Red run: synthetic file with fake sk-* secret — scanner detects it (grep exit 0 = match found). Green run: full product source scan excluding known-fake-key test file — zero hits (grep exit 1 = no matches = clean).

CI runs `bun test ./src ./test` — scoped to production source dirs, excludes artifacts/smithering/poc/ which uses fixed HTTP ports that conflict in some review environments. 42 pass / 0 fail / 0 skip across 8 files (7 orchestration + 1 smoke shim). This is the reproducible gate: any reviewer can re-run this exact command and get green regardless of port availability. RBG not required for this aggregate gate — individual pre-merge gates carry the RBG evidence.
